key_issues: Batch is ~two weeks, not ~one: the queue delivers ALL of Stage S1 plus ALL of Stage S2 (M3, M4, M5a-e are the entirety of S2's modules, not an 'early head start'). Re-scope to S1-only (queue 002 takes S2), or split the oversized items and trim the S2 tail.

Item 003 too large: structs+contracts for every request/response/notification/error across BOTH spec revisions is multi-day. Split into one item per revision (2025-11-25; 2026-07-28 incl. _meta envelope), each with its own round-trip + contract-rejection tests.

Item 007 bundles two modules (M3 provider port + the hand-rolled default JSON-Schema provider) and is too large. Split into provider.rkt (port + stub conformance) and from-json-schema.rkt (keyword subset + Ajv-cross-checked accept/reject suite).

Item 010 lumps three things of very different size/cohesion: M5d auth structs (tiny), M5e stdio framing (non-trivial, lone I/O module), and the S2 demo (a stage-closeout artifact depending on items 007/009/010). Separate them; make the S2 demo its own closeout item.

Non-blocking: item 006 triple-duties (barrels + portability load test + S1 demo) but is acceptable as the S1 closeout; flag only. Ensure the typescript-sdk/ checkout is actually available to the Worker, since several acceptance criteria assert byte-for-byte/Ajv parity against it.
